hooks.prompt: Before context compaction, automatically log this session. Check if .my-ops-config.json exists in the project root. If it does, follow the session-log skill: read sessionLog.destination ('notion', 'obsidian', or 'both'), summarize the conversation, collect changed files (git diff --name-only), recent commits (git log --oneline --since='today'), and lessons learned. Then write to the configured destination(s) — create a Notion page using the notion settings and/or write a markdown file into the Obsidian vault using the obsidian settings. If .my-ops-config.json does not exist, skip logging silently.
